The scene cuts to another man, in a black hoodie and beanie, intensely eating from a white plate with a fork, making slurping sounds and exclaiming "Mmm" as a progress bar and '805 CALORIES' are displayed. During this, a voice playfully exclaims "He's wearing my box, man, get out my box, it's my box!" and another comments that "Ken doesn't know how to bend his arms that way." The video returns to the original room with the brick wall and blue lighting, where the man in the black hoodie eating a sandwich is now joined by the man in the black hoodie with the British flag icon, both seated at a table with microphones, laughing and interacting. After another voice responds, "I don't know," he assures, "We'll find out, I'll let you know." He then initiates a toast, "Hey, cheers boys, cheers," adding "Cheers and cookies as well," and lamenting, "We've gone... We've fallen so far," concluding, "It's too much. I don't want but it's too much." The scene quickly transitions to the Man in white t-shirt with a small 'OFF' logo, now in a brightly lit mall environment, holding a large chocolate chip cookie and speaking to the camera. He takes a bite, and on-screen text appears: '260 CALORIES'. He is heard saying, "You're going to eat anymore," and "It's good," before continuing, "You need to realize, it's 3 PM. I've eaten more calories than I do the whole day." A close-up of the cookie reveals its texture and white chocolate chunks. The Man in white t-shirt continues to eat, while the Man in white bucket hat, wearing a white bucket hat with a black Adidas logo and a black crossbody strap, appears beside him, also holding a cookie. On-screen text reads 'SHUT UP AND EAT YOUR COOKIE' and 'NO ONE CARES, EAT YOUR COOKIE', adding a humorous tone. The Man in white bucket hat tells him, "You're going to eat your cookie," and repeatedly urges, "Come here, come here, come here." The Man in white t-shirt responds, "It deserves it, deserves it," and later justifies, "For the content, for the content, for the content." The Man in white bucket hat asks, "What did he do?" then expresses, "I love that," and comments, "That was rude." The Man in white t-shirt replies, "Oh, I don't deserve that," to which the Man in white bucket hat declares, "I'll do it all." The Man in white t-shirt ponders, "I can't work out what flavor it is. Is it cinnamon?" and the Man in white bucket hat confirms, "Yeah," with the Man in white t-shirt repeating, "Cinnamon." The Man in white bucket hat begins to say, "But this is a..." as the segment ends. The Man in white t-shirt reacts with exaggerated expressions, laughing and gesturing, while the Man in white bucket hat listens and occasionally responds. The background shows a bustling mall with various stores, including a visible 'VALENTINO' sign, and people walking by, contributing to the lighthearted, comedic atmosphere as the two men interact playfully, with the Man in white t-shirt taking another bite and showing the cookie to the camera, and the Man in white bucket hat gesturing and speaking.

He details his recent food order, which included "naked chicken, seven naked chickens, loaded waffle fries, and the chicken burger," totaling an estimated "3,000 calories," bringing his overall intake to "5,025 calories." He explains he's recording a video to reply to Alex Wassabi, having been unable to respond earlier due to being in Vegas. The scene then cuts to a dimly lit room with a dark brick wall and blue backlighting, where a food challenge appears to be underway, indicated by an on-screen progress bar with chicken wing icons and calorie counts. Here, a man in a black hoodie with a British flag icon beside him expresses disbelief, stating, "I can't believe I've just trained a burger," and lists other items like "six tenders and waffle fries," later adding, "this belly right here is full." Another man in a black shirt with pink and blue stripes, eating a burger, echoes his sentiment, saying, "I can't believe it either," and later exclaims, "Oh, bro!" The man in the black hoodie then addresses someone named "Simon," remarking, "Every calorie is a calorie, Simon," before asking a fourth man, "Ethan, you having a you having a Coke?" This fourth man, wearing a striped black, pink, and blue t-shirt with tattoos, is seen holding a red Coca-Cola can and smiling, confirming, "I'm having a Coke." The man in the black hoodie observes the can's black logo, asking, "Wait, but the logo's black," to which he clarifies, "No, no, no, no, this new edition. Black edition. This Coke." The casual and energetic atmosphere is further enhanced by the presence of a 'SIDEMEN' sign, a microphone, and decorative shelves, suggesting a content creation session.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a dimly lit studio with a dark brick wall and blue backlighting, where the Man in black hoodie with a British flag icon and the Man in striped black, pink, and blue t-shirt are seated at a table with microphones. Both hold red Coca-Cola cans. The Man in black hoodie, speaking with a distinct British accent, teases his companion, asking "Why is your one white?" and then, after reading a label, declares "That's a 36 grams of sugar on that." The Man in striped t-shirt, also with a distinct British accent, sips his drink, laughs, and then exclaims "Oh my god, it's got zero!" upon realizing it's Coke Zero. The Man in black hoodie continues to tease, saying "It's got zero... and you're drinking it for nothing." This lighthearted banter quickly turns dramatic as the Man in striped t-shirt begins to cough and choke loudly, prompting exclamations of "Oh no!" and "No, no, no" from both, followed by the Man in black hoodie jokingly declaring "The set's ruined!" amidst the choking sounds. After recovering, the Man in striped t-shirt quips, "I never want to be carried for you." He then stands up, leans over the table, and looks down at something off-screen, possibly a phone or camera. The scene abruptly shifts to a brightly lit mall environment, where upbeat hip-hop music begins to play. The Man in white t-shirt with a small 'OFF' logo is now sporting large white pig-shaped novelty sunglasses and a white bucket hat, smiling and interacting with the Man in Pikachu hat and green sunglasses, who is also smiling and wearing yellow Pikachu-themed sunglasses. As they walk through the mall, passing storefronts including one labeled 'CANDLE', the Man in white t-shirt prompts his companion, "Hey man, what you about, man? Tell him why you got the tattoo." The Man in Pikachu hat, laughing and gesturing, repeatedly states "We always come correct" and then begins to recount a story about an encounter in Compton, describing how "some guy from Compton decided to G-check me... he was just talking to me about Compton." The atmosphere changes from a controlled studio setting to a playful, public, and vibrant mall space, emphasizing humor and fun through exaggerated accessories and casual interaction.

The scene then shifts to a studio setting, where two men are seated at a table with microphones and a 'SIDEMEN' sign in the background. One, the Man in black hoodie with British flag icon, holds a red Coca-Cola can and speaks with a distinct British accent, discussing how "you guys on the UK" might be "moving correct" or "want to get into a fight." The other, the Man in striped black, pink, and blue t-shirt, also holding a Coca-Cola can, laughs and responds with a British accent, recalling a humorous situation about "G-checking someone" and how "he can't read us." He emphatically states, "from East London, I always come correct," a phrase that thematically links to the 'Come Correct' shirt seen earlier. He continues, describing how "he loved it" and suggested coming to Miami. The Man in black hoodie then mentions "big blow Vic from Sheffield," before the Man in striped t-shirt reiterates, "London, I come correct, man," followed by laughter. A distinct, almost commercial-like male voice then describes "drumstick squishy time for Bazinger," detailing "160 grams of the good stuff" and asking "What flavor we got today?" He warns not to drop them, as "that's calories." The Man in black hoodie interjects, "Get off my coloring pack," before the commercial voice identifies "Sour cherry and apple." The Man in black hoodie confirms, "Oh, they're really good," and the Man in striped t-shirt adds, "Yeah, in his mouth," leading to more laughter. The camera then pans to show a wider view of the studio, revealing a large white light and a dark brick wall. The scene transitions to a different part of the studio where the Man in striped black, pink, and blue t-shirt is seen juggling a white and black soccer ball, while the Man in black hoodie watches intently. As the ball is juggled, both men exclaim "Oh!" and "A dolphin!" in surprise and amusement. The Man in black hoodie remarks, "Wow, that was mad." The segment concludes with a new male voice stating, "My name's Harry." The overall atmosphere is lively and humorous, with the men engaging in playful interactions and enjoying each other's company.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a man in a white and black jacket standing in a vibrant neon-lit urban environment at night, speaking to the camera, admitting he's "4,000 calories in and not feeling good," and "not built for this kind of life." The scene quickly shifts to a daytime outdoor park setting where the 'Man in black hoodie with British flag icon' playfully dances and crouches near a stone fountain, holding a McDonald’s box and a chicken nugget, with a progress bar showing chicken wing icons and calorie counts at the bottom. He exclaims, "Look, you wouldn't believe it," and mentions dropping half his food but still feeling full, followed by gasping, laughter, and repeatedly stating, "I might be having a heart attack," after dropping the box, spilling nuggets and ketchup onto the ground. The video transitions to a McDonald’s interior at night, where the 'Man in black jersey with Rasta stripes' and the 'Man in black hoodie and beanie' are seated at a table, sipping colorful drinks—green, orange, and dark soda—while eating chicken nuggets and fries. The 'Man in black hoodie with British flag icon' encourages, "Get the last one down, you're a hero," and announces a "Progress report. We're almost there." The 'Man in black jersey with Rasta stripes' takes a sip of his green drink, smiling, as the camera captures close-ups of the food and drinks, commenting on the drink, "I don't know how I'm doing the drink," while another voice urges him to "down that chug." He then praises the drink, saying, "It's really good though, like really good. It's one of the best drinks I've ever had in my life," and adds, "What a great life I've had." The scene then cuts to an outdoor urban street where two men, including the 'Man in black hoodie with British flag icon', are walking and drinking from cups, one holding a white cup with a straw, while the other gestures animatedly. The 'Man in black hoodie with British flag icon' announces, "Jeez, we're stepping out, the boys," and states they're "just having a good time, you know, chilling." The final shot shows them walking down a city street with buildings and trees in the background, under overcast daylight, as the camera pans to follow their movement, all set to upbeat, energetic background music.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with an energetic electronic music track, featuring a driving beat and synth melodies, setting a dynamic and futuristic tone. Visually, a neon-lit animation unfolds, showcasing floating black boxes with the 'SDMN' logo and the word 'TONE' in large, glowing letters, all set against a vibrant pink and blue lit brick wall backdrop. The scene then transitions to a man in a white hoodie with a colorful graphic on the back, performing energetic movements under intense blue and purple lighting, subtly revealing the 'SDMN' logo on his socks and a black and white striped shirt. The high-energy electronic music continues as the setting shifts to a futuristic, high-tech retail space. This environment features a central runway flanked by illuminated display pedestals showcasing various clothing items, while large screens on either side display models wearing SDMN apparel. A group of men poses confidently on the central stage, bathed in neon pink and cyan lights, with a prominent 'SDMN' logo displayed above. The website 'SIDEMEN.CLOTHING.COM' is visible on the runway, along with a credit to 'EDIT BY @Z68VFX' in the bottom right, before the sequence concludes with a black screen, still accompanied by the continuous, driving music.
